evidence: SemanticFieldExplorer.exploreMultiSeed declares @NonNull Set<String> seeds but immediately follows with Objects.requireNonNull(seeds, 'seeds must not be null') — the annotation promises non-null at call site, yet the body treats that promise as untrustworthy.

CollocateQueryHelper exposes a forTesting() factory that sets this.index = null despite the primary constructor advertising @NonNull BlackLabIndex; the only enforcement is a verbal javadoc warning that NPE will be thrown — the type system cannot distinguish test instances from production ones.

BlackLabSnippetParser methods that return null are consistently annotated @Nullable (extractPosFromMatch, extractCollocateFromXmlByPosition, detokenize, extractLastLemma, safeParts); callers (CollocateQueryHelper.toHitRecord, extractCollocateLemma) check before use. Strong local contract coherence.

QueryExecutor.executeCollocations declares @Nullable String lemma and documents 'Returns an empty list silently if null or empty'; BlackLabQueryExecutor implementation honours this exactly — null/empty lemma returns Collections.emptyList() without logging an error.

executeDependencyPattern declares @Nullable String headPosConstraint with documentation 'pass null to skip the constraint'; the implementation guards with null before building the POS constraint — annotation and body agree.

GrammarConfigLoader.parseRelationType is annotated @Nullable and returns null for blank/null input, documented clearly; callers pass the result straight into the RelationConfig constructor which accepts it.

ExplorationService interface @throws Javadoc mentions ExplorationException but the signature does not declare it (unchecked RuntimeException); the implementation adds 'throws ExplorationException' to each override for IDE-level documentation. No mismatch — the design choice is deliberate and documented in ExplorationException's class javadoc.
CollocateQueryHelper.getTotalFrequency documents 'Returns 0L if the lemma annotation is not found' and 'throws IOException wrapping any RuntimeException' — both paths are implemented faithfully.

contract_coherence.dimension_character: Contract coherence is broadly strong: nullable returns carry @Nullable annotations, null-accepting parameters are documented and honoured, and the two-tier IOException/IllegalArgumentException exception strategy in GrammarConfigLoader is precisely specified and implemented. The two persistent weaknesses are both in the exploration/query boundary: a redundant null-guard that directly contradicts a @NonNull annotation, and a test-escape factory that creates an intentionally broken instance whose behavioural limits exist only in prose, not in the type system.
contract_coherence.score_rationale: The majority of the API surface — query ports, snippet parser, config loader, exploration options — shows deliberate, consistent contracts that developers can trust. The score is held below 80 by two confirmed, still-open issues that are representative of the same root pattern: a @NonNull annotation that is not fully believed (explicit null check) and a type-system bypass for test instances with a verbal-only safety contract. Neither issue is widespread, but both affect the boundary between the exploration and query subsystems, which is a high-traffic path.

issues.summary: @NonNull seeds annotation contradicted by explicit null-check in exploreMultiSeed body
evidence: Method signature: public @NonNull ExplorationResult exploreMultiSeed(@NonNull Set<String> seeds, ...)
First line of body: Objects.requireNonNull(seeds, "seeds must not be null");

@NonNull annotation signals to callers and static analysers that null must never be passed; the body then treats that guarantee as insufficient and re-checks it, sending conflicting signals about whether null is a real risk.
issues.suggestion: Remove the Objects.requireNonNull call — the @NonNull annotation is the authoritative contract. If defensive null-guarding is desired at this boundary, replace @NonNull with @Nullable and let the requireNonNull throw be the only enforcement, so annotation and behaviour agree.

issues.summary: CollocateQueryHelper.forTesting() creates an instance with null index; type system cannot enforce the verbal-only usage contract
evidence: Primary constructor: CollocateQueryHelper(@NonNull BlackLabIndex index) — @NonNull signals index is always valid.
Private no-arg constructor: this.index = null — directly contradicts @NonNull.
forTesting() factory: returns new CollocateQueryHelper() with index=null.

Javadoc warns 'Any method that delegates to the BlackLab index will throw NullPointerException if called on this instance'; this contract is verbal only — the type system cannot distinguish a test instance from a production one.
issues.suggestion: Introduce a narrow test-only interface or subclass that overrides only the purely computational methods used in tests, leaving index-dependent methods unimplemented/throwing UnsupportedOperationException with a clear message. Alternatively, pass the index as Optional<BlackLabIndex> or use a guard method (assertIndexAvailable()) with a descriptive IllegalStateException instead of a raw NPE, so that miscalling a test instance produces a clear error rather than an opaque NullPointerException.

root_causes: Annotation-body mismatch pattern: @NonNull annotations added for static analysis benefit, but developers retain defensive null checks from earlier un-annotated code — both issues share this root cause.
likely_symptoms: nonnull_seeds_with_requirenonnull — symptom of defensive-coding habit persisting after annotation adoption
collocate_query_helper_null_index_verbal — symptom of using a private constructor escape hatch rather than a proper test double

add.header: Nullable return contracts consistently annotated in parser layer
add.description: BlackLabSnippetParser methods that may return null (extractPosFromMatch, safeParts, extractCollocateFromXmlByPosition, etc.) are all annotated @Nullable; callers perform null checks before use. This is a positive pattern that removes guesswork at call sites.

add.header: Two-tier IOException / IllegalArgumentException strategy in config layer
add.description: GrammarConfigLoader documents and implements a deliberate two-tier exception strategy: IOException for I/O failures, IllegalArgumentException for structural/validation failures. This distinction makes error handling predictable for callers.

add.header: Nullable parameter contracts honoured in executeCollocations and executeDependencyPattern
add.description: @Nullable lemma / headPosConstraint parameters are documented with explicit behaviour ('returns empty list silently', 'pass null to skip') and implementations match those contracts exactly.
